text:
There was once a peculiar old librarian named Harold Owl. He spent countless nights reading obscure poetry instead of actually borrowing anything. One moonlit night, Harold Owl fell asleep reading an ancient poem about an owl spying on a lover. Suddenly, he could hear soft rustling. Peering out his window, he saw a beautiful golden-eyed owl perched on an ancient oak.

"You speak owl?" the owl asked gently.

"What?), oh. Just an old squire. You?"

"I'm Lady twilight. These woods are starlit."

"Hmmm." They exchanged some words. Lady twilight fluttered down.

"You’ve read? These woods hold secrets."

"I’m fond of secrets."

They discussed literature all night. By dawn, Lady twilight had several lost poems hers. Harold Owl cherished them.

The next autumn, Harold Owl fell ill. Lady twilight hovered beside him.

"I have some medicine for owls," she whispered softly.

With those words, she swooped down. She gave Harold Owl a bottle filled
